name: Drupada
description: Drupada, also known as Yajnasena, was the king of Panchala in the epic Mahabharata. His birth was miraculous, arising from a sacrificial fire performed by the Brahmins Yaja and Upayaja, who sought to avenge Drona's humiliation of Drupada. Drupada and Drona were childhood friends, but Drona, due to his poverty, was insulted by Drupada who declared that friendship could only exist between equals. This incident fueled Drona's resentment and later became a pivotal point in the Mahabharata war. Drupada vowed to avenge this insult and sought a son who could kill Drona. From the same fire, Dhrishtadyumna, destined to kill Drona, and Draupadi, the future wife of the Pandavas, were born. Drupada organized Draupadi's swayamvara, a contest where she would choose her husband. Arjuna, disguised as a Brahmin, won the contest, leading to Draupadi's polyandrous marriage to the five Pandava brothers. Drupada played a crucial role in the Kurukshetra War, siding with the Pandavas and serving as a commander in their army. He was ultimately killed by Drona, fulfilling the prophecy associated with Dhrishtadyumna's birth and highlighting the themes of fate, revenge, and dharma within the Mahabharata. His character symbolizes the consequences of pride and the enduring power of friendship and enmity.
subtitle: King of Panchala, father of Draupadi
achievements: Fathered Dhrishtadyumna, destined to kill Drona
Led the Panchala army in the Kurukshetra War
Forged an alliance with the Pandavas
Avenge his humiliation by Drona (through his children)

keyMyths.title: Birth from the Fire Sacrifice
keyMyths.description: Drupada was born from a sacrificial fire performed by Brahmins to avenge his humiliation by Drona. From the same fire arose Dhrishtadyumna and Draupadi, destined for significant roles in the Kurukshetra War. This miraculous birth highlights the theme of divine intervention and preordained destiny.

keyMyths.title: Draupadi's Swayamvara
keyMyths.description: Drupada organized a swayamvara for his daughter Draupadi, where she would choose her husband. Arjuna, disguised as a Brahmin, successfully completed the challenge, winning her hand and setting in motion the events leading to the Pandavas' alliance with Drupada.

keyMyths.title: Death at the Hands of Drona
keyMyths.description: Drupada fought valiantly on the side of the Pandavas in the Kurukshetra War, but was ultimately killed by Drona. This fulfilled the prophecy surrounding Dhrishtadyumna's birth, as he was destined to avenge his father's death by slaying Drona.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: The primary source for Drupada's story, detailing his birth, life, and role in the Kurukshetra War.
sources.text: The primary source for Drupada's story, detailing his birth, life, and role in the Kurukshetra War.

sources.description: A popular retelling of the Mahabharata, providing a comprehensive account of Drupada's character and significance.
